---
title: "Draft: Data Documentation"
---
```{r}
library(dplyr)

watertemp<- read.csv("phys_ocean_data.csv")
watertemp<- watertemp|> group_by(Date)
wtc<- watertemp |> count(Water.Temp...F.)
```


1. Who collected the data?
  NOAA has stations in which they have meteorological sensors.
2. Why the data was collected?
  They collect the data for the use of analyzing sea level trends.
3. What the data is about?
  The data gives a 31 day range giving the temperatures at different points of the day.
4. When the data was collected.?
  The data is calculated throughout the year, but there missing data for specific months
5. Where the data was collected?
  The data was collected from their Dauphin Island, AL station (ID: 8735180).
6. How the data was generated/collected?
  The meteorlogical sensors collect the data by using contact based probes in intervals of 6 minutes.
7. Structure of the data?
  The data is structured where you can find temperature conductivity for each interval of time collected.
8. Formatting decisions in the data?
  They decided that for formatting you would only be able to get data in intervals of 31 days per csv file.
9. Data validation/quality control
  For validating data there are 3 algorithims that collect and process data and then are compared against satellite data.
10. How the data can be reused/license
  Due to the freedom of Information Act any person has the ritght to obtain records
11. Suggested data analysis methods
  NOAA use Optimum Interpolation Sea Surface Temperature, which uses a variety of ways that collects data and puts it in a global grid.
12. Measurement instruments used
  Meteorological Sensors

Codebook
1. Variable name in the code?
  Water.Temp...F.
2. Long-form description of what was measured
  Water temperature for the given dates
3. Units of measurement
  Farenheit
4. Acceptable values
  All temperatures measured
5. Values used to indicate missingness, refusal to respond, etc.
  Values have a - to indicate missingness
6. Additional notes that may be relevant
  This data is between July 22nd to August 22nd due to it having a higher frequency at which it would be higher than the given maximum. It may be beneficial to get more date ranges to get a scope on the rest of the year, but NOAA only allows for 31 day range per csv.
